questions.questionText: A photon of energy $h\nu$ is absorbed by a free electron of a metal having work function $w < h\nu$. Then :
options: The electron is sure to come out
The electron is sure to come out with a kinetic energy $(h\nu - w)$
Either the electron does not come out or it comes with a kinetic energy $(h\nu - w)$
It may come out with a kinetic energy less than $(h\nu - w)$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A 40 W electric bulb emits light of wavelength 620 nm and works with 80% efficiency. Find the number of photons emitted by the bulb in 20 seconds. (1 eV = $1.6 \times 10^{-19}$ J, $hc = 12400$ eV Å)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Consider two atoms represented as $z_1A^{M_1}$ and $z_2B^{M_2}$. Given that $M_1 \neq M_2$ and $Z_1 \neq Z_2$, but the difference $(M_1 - Z_1)$ is equal to $(M_2 - Z_2)$, these two species are classified as
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Moseley's equation for the determination of wavelength of X-rays is (v = frequency of wave, Z = nuclear charge, a and b are constants)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Light of wavelength $\lambda$ shines on a metal surface with intensity $X$, and the metal emits $Y$ electrons per second of average energy $Z$. What will happen to $Y$ and $Z$ if $X$ is doubled?
options: $Y$ will be doubled and $Z$ will become half.
$Y$ will remain same and $Z$ will be doubled.
$Y$ will be doubled but $Z$ will remain same.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a photoelectric effect setup, silver metal emits photoelectrons when light of wavelength 256.7 nm falls on it. It is found that applying a stopping potential of 0.35 V just prevents the photoelectrons from reaching the collector. Determine the work function of silver.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The radius of a hydrogen atom is approximately 0.05 nm, while the radius of its proton (nucleus) is $1.5 \times 10^{-15}$ m. Treating both the hydrogen atom and the proton as spheres, find the fraction of the total volume of a hydrogen atom that is occupied by its nucleus.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Each Br2 molecule in the gaseous state absorbs one quantum of light to dissociate into Br atoms. If the absorbed radiation has wavelength 5000 Å, find the energy needed in kJ/mol.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Photoelectric emission is observed from two surfaces at incident frequencies $ u_1$ and $ u_2$ ($ u_1 > u_2$). Maximum kinetic energies of the photoelectrons emitted in the above two cases are in the ratio of $1 : k$. If threshold frequency in both cases is $ u_0$, then this value is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A light source of wavelength $\lambda$ illuminates a metal and ejects photo-electrons with $(K.E.)_{\text{max}} = 1 \text{ eV}$

Another light source of wavelength $\frac{\lambda}{3}$ ejects photo-electrons from same metal with $(K.E.)_{\text{max}} = 4 \text{ eV}$

Find the value of work function?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Light of wavelength $\lambda$ is incident on a metal surface and the emitted photoelectrons have a maximum kinetic energy of 1 eV. When another light of wavelength $\frac{\lambda}{3}$ falls on the same metal, the maximum kinetic energy of the emitted photoelectrons becomes 4 eV. The work function of the metal is to be written in the form $x \times 10^{-1}\,\mathrm{eV}$, where $x$ is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An electron starts from rest and is accelerated through a potential difference of 40 kV. Its de Broglie wavelength is $x \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~m}$. Find the value of $x$ (Nearest integer).

Given: Mass of electron $=9.1 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{~kg}$

Charge of an electron $=1.6 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{C}$

Planck constant $=6.63 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A monochromatic light source of wavelength 400 nm emits 1000 J energy in 10 s. This radiation is incident on a sodium surface and causes emission of $x \times 10^{20}$ electrons per second. Take that 400 nm radiation has enough energy to just produce photoelectrons from sodium. The value of $x$ is

$\_\_\_\_$ . (Nearest integer) $\left(h=6.626 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}\right)$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Radiation with a wavelength of 500 nm falls on a metallic surface. If the threshold frequency required for the photoelectric effect to occur on this metal is $4.3 \times 10^{14} \mathrm{~Hz}$, determine the speed of the emitted photoelectron as $\_\_\_\_$ $\times 10^5 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$.

(Round off to the nearest integer)

(Given: Planck's constant $h=6.63 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}$, mass of electron $m_e=9.0 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{~kg}$)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The work function for a metal is 40 eV . To emit photo electrons of zero velocity from the surface of the metal the wavelength of incident light should be x nm .
questions.subject: Chemistry
